title: Complaints and requests in person
description: Make polite complaints and requests face-to-face â€” at hotels, shops, restaurants, and offices â€” and handle responses professionally.

content.title: How to make a polite complaint
content.body:
A good complaint has THREE parts:
1. EXPLAIN the problem clearly
2. GIVE a reason why it matters
3. REQUEST a solution

Useful language:
â€¢ 'I'm afraid there's a problem with...'
â€¢ 'I'd like to bring something to your attention.'
â€¢ 'I'm not entirely happy with...'
â€¢ 'I think there may have been a mistake with...'
â€¢ 'I was expecting... but instead...'
â€¢ 'This isn't quite what I ordered / asked for.'

Requesting a solution:
â€¢ 'Could you possibly...?'
â€¢ 'I'd appreciate it if you could...'
â€¢ 'Would it be possible to...?'
â€¢ 'I'd like a replacement / refund / correction, please.'
content.grammar_note:
Polite complaints use 'I'm afraid' to soften
'I'm not entirely happy' = softer than 'This is terrible'
Always request a solution, not just complain

content.title: Handling a complaint â€” the staff side
content.body:
If you work in service and receive a complaint, respond professionally:

ACKNOWLEDGING:
â€¢ 'I understand completely â€” I'm very sorry about that.'
â€¢ 'Thank you for bringing this to our attention.'
â€¢ 'I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience.'

OFFERING SOLUTIONS:
â€¢ 'I'll look into this right away.'
â€¢ 'I can offer you a replacement / refund / discount.'
â€¢ 'Let me speak to the manager for you.'
â€¢ 'We'll make sure this doesn't happen again.'

NEVER say:
âœ— 'That's not my problem.'
âœ— 'You should have checked before.'
âœ— 'I can't do anything about it.'
content.grammar_note:
Always acknowledge before offering a solution
'I'll look into this' = I will investigate immediately
Never blame the customer

vocabulary.example: The charger I bought is defective â€” it doesn't work.
I think I've been overcharged â€” the bill seems too high.

vocabulary.meaning: money given back after returning a product
vocabulary.example: I'd like a full refund, please.

vocabulary.meaning: to swap a product for another
vocabulary.example: Could I exchange this for a larger size?

vocabulary.meaning: to take a problem to a higher level
vocabulary.example: If this isn't resolved, I'll need to escalate the complaint.

vocabulary.meaning: the solution to a problem or complaint
vocabulary.example: Thank you â€” I'm happy with this resolution.

sentences: I'm afraid there's a problem with my room â€” the air conditioning isn't working.
I think there may have been a mistake with my bill â€” I was charged for a dish I didn't order.
I understand completely â€” I'll look into this right away.
Could you possibly send someone to fix it as soon as possible?
I'd like to return this â€” it seems to be defective.

content.question: You ordered a vegetarian meal but received a non-vegetarian one. Which is the most polite complaint?
options: This is wrong! Take it back!
Excuse me â€” I'm afraid this isn't what I ordered. I asked for the vegetarian option. Could you check and replace it, please?
I hate this food.
Wrong food. Change it.
content.correct: Excuse me â€” I'm afraid this isn't what I ordered. I asked for the vegetarian option. Could you check and replace it, please?
content.explanation: 'I'm afraid' softens the complaint. State the problem clearly, reference your original request, and politely ask for a solution.

content.question: You bought a pair of headphones and one side doesn't work. What do you say at the shop?
options: These headphones are broken! Give me money back!
Excuse me â€” I bought these headphones yesterday and I'm afraid one side appears to be defective. I'd like to exchange them or get a refund, please.
This doesn't work.
I want to complain about everything in this shop.
content.correct: Excuse me â€” I bought these headphones yesterday and I'm afraid one side appears to be defective. I'd like to exchange them or get a refund, please.
content.explanation: Polite structure: context (when you bought) + problem ('I'm afraid' + defective) + clear request (exchange or refund).

content.question: A customer complains that their hotel room was not cleaned. The best staff response is:
options: You should have called earlier.
That's housekeeping's fault, not mine.
I sincerely apologise for this â€” that's certainly not the standard we aim for. I'll send housekeeping up immediately.
The room was cleaned â€” you must be wrong.
content.correct: I sincerely apologise for this â€” that's certainly not the standard we aim for. I'll send housekeeping up immediately.
content.explanation: Acknowledge, apologise, reassure, and offer immediate action. Never blame the customer or defend poorly.

content.question: You check your restaurant bill and notice you've been charged for a dessert you didn't have. What do you say?
options: You made a mistake!
Excuse me â€” I think there might be an error on my bill. I see a charge for dessert, but I don't believe we ordered any. Could you double-check?
The bill is wrong. Change it.
I'm not paying this.
content.correct: Excuse me â€” I think there might be an error on my bill. I see a charge for dessert, but I don't believe we ordered any. Could you double-check?
content.explanation: 'I think there might be an error' = polite framing. 'I don't believe' = less accusatory than 'you made a mistake'.

content.instruction: Fill in the correct complaint or resolution phrase.

sentences.text: 'I'm ___ there's a problem with my order â€” it seems to be missing an item.'
'I ___ like a replacement or a full refund, please.'
'Thank you for ___ this to our attention â€” I'll sort it immediately.'
'I was ___ for two coffees but I only ordered one.'
'Could you ___ send someone to check the issue?'
Guest: '___ there seems to be a problem with the hot water in my room.' (Excuse me)
Staff: 'I ___ â€” I'll send maintenance right away.' (apologise sincerely)
Guest: 'Also, I ___ I was charged for breakfast I didn't have.' (think â€” mistake)
sentences.answer: think there may have been a mistake â€”

sentences.text: Staff: 'Let me ___ the bill and correct it immediately.' (check)
Guest: 'Thank you â€” I appreciate your ___ response.' (quick)

content.instruction: Arrange into a polite complaint sentence.
content.answer: I'm afraid the heating is not quite working.

content.instruction: Arrange into a resolution offer from staff.
content.answer: I'll look into this right away.

content.instruction: Match the complaint to the appropriate solution response.

pairs.right: I sincerely apologise â€” I'll send housekeeping up immediately.

pairs.left: 'I've been charged for something I didn't order.'
pairs.right: I'll check the bill and correct it right away.
I can offer you an exchange or a full refund.

pairs.left: 'The food took over an hour to arrive.'
pairs.right: I'm very sorry for the wait â€” let me offer a complimentary dessert.

pairs.left: 'The Wi-Fi password I was given doesn't work.'
pairs.right: Let me reset it for you â€” here's the updated password.

content.title: â˜… FEATURED DIALOGUE â€” Scenario 4: Hotel complaint and resolution â€” Chennai
content.setting: Kavya checks into a business hotel in Chennai. Several things are not right with her room and she needs to sort them out. This dialogue features polite complaints, professional staff responses, and a successful resolution.
content.learning_note: Notice how Kavya stays calm and specific throughout â€” and how the staff responds with genuine solutions. This scenario is revisited in Lesson 13.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Excuse me â€” I checked in about an hour ago. I'm in Room 412.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Yes of course â€” how can I help you, Ms Iyer?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: I'm afraid there are a couple of issues with my room. First â€” the air conditioning isn't cooling properly. It's quite warm in there.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: I sincerely apologise for that. I'll send our maintenance team up right away to check it.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Thank you. The second issue is â€” I requested a non-smoking room when I booked, but the room smells strongly of smoke.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Oh â€” I'm very sorry about that. That's certainly not acceptable. Would you like us to move you to a different room?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Yes, I think that would be best. Could you possibly find me a room on a higher floor? I'd prefer the view as well if that's possible.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Of course â€” let me check availability. We do have Room 608 on the sixth floor â€” it's a deluxe room with a city view. I can move you there at no extra charge.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: That sounds perfect. One more thing â€” I was charged for airport pickup in my bill but I actually made my own way here.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: I'll look into that right away. I can see there may have been a mix-up with the booking. I'll remove that charge from your bill immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Thank you â€” I appreciate that. And could I also request extra towels to be sent to the new room?

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Absolutely â€” I'll have them sent up along with a welcome amenity as our apology for the inconvenience.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: That's very kind. Thank you for handling this so efficiently â€” I appreciate it.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.text: Of course, Ms Iyer. We want to make sure your stay is as comfortable as possible. I'll arrange for your bags to be moved to Room 608 within the next 15 minutes.

comprehension_questions.question: What are the THREE problems Kavya reports?
options: Bad food, broken TV, wrong room
AC not cooling, smoky room, incorrect charge on bill
No hot water, smoky room, wrong floor
Dirty room, AC problem, slow room service
comprehension_questions.correct: AC not cooling, smoky room, incorrect charge on bill

comprehension_questions.question: What room is Kavya moved to?
options: Room 412
Room 504
Room 608
Room 412 after fixing

comprehension_questions.question: What extra does the hotel offer as an apology?
options: A free dinner
A late checkout
A welcome amenity
A room upgrade fee waived and free dinner

discussion_questions: What three complaint phrases does Kavya use? How does each one stay polite?
What makes the receptionist's responses professional? List 3 things.
How does the conversation end positively despite the problems?

content.instruction: Find and correct the complaint language mistake.

sentences.wrong: This food is disgusting â€” you should be ashamed!
sentences.correct: Excuse me â€” I'm afraid this dish doesn't taste quite right. Could you check if everything is okay with it?
sentences.explanation: Personal attacks and extreme language make complaints worse. Stay calm and specific.

sentences.correct: Staff response: 'Let me find the right person to help you â€” I'll connect you to the manager right away.'
sentences.explanation: Good service = never pass the buck. Take ownership and connect them to a solution even if it's not directly your responsibility.

sentences.wrong: I want to make a complaint about everything.
sentences.correct: Excuse me â€” I'd like to raise a concern about [specific issue], please.
sentences.explanation: Complaints should be specific and focused. 'Everything' is vague and unhelpful.

content.scenario: You ordered a product online and it arrived damaged. You call customer service. What do you say?
options: Your company is terrible! The product arrived broken!
Hello â€” I recently placed an order and I'm afraid the product arrived damaged. The packaging was intact but the item inside was cracked. Could you arrange a replacement or refund, please?
Damaged item. Want money back.
This is unacceptable and I'll never order again.
content.correct: Hello â€” I recently placed an order and I'm afraid the product arrived damaged. The packaging was intact but the item inside was cracked. Could you arrange a replacement or refund, please?
content.explanation: Polite, specific complaint: 'I'm afraid' + clear description of damage + specific request for resolution.

content.instruction: Role-play a restaurant complaint. You've been waiting 45 minutes for your food. When it arrives, it's cold and the wrong order. Make a polite but firm complaint and request a resolution.
content.prompt: Make a polite restaurant complaint covering: the wait time, the wrong food, the temperature, and your requested resolution.
content.example_response: Excuse me â€” I'm afraid there are a couple of issues I need to raise. Firstly, we've been waiting nearly 45 minutes for our food â€” the expected time was 20 minutes. We were quite worried that the order had been forgotten. Secondly, when the food arrived, I'm afraid this isn't what I ordered. I asked for the paneer tikka masala, but this appears to be a different dish â€” I think it might be the butter chicken. And unfortunately, the food has also arrived quite cold, which suggests it may have been sitting for a while. I completely understand that you're busy today and I don't want to make a scene â€” but I'd like to request that you replace my dish with the correct one, freshly made and hot. It would also be very much appreciated if the manager could check on this. I've been a regular here for years and I know this isn't the standard you normally maintain.
key_phrases: I'm afraid there are a couple of issues
I asked for... but this appears to be...
I'd like to request that you replace

content.instruction: Now role-play the OTHER side â€” you are the restaurant manager. A customer comes to you with the complaint from Task 17. Handle it professionally.
content.prompt: As a restaurant manager, respond to a customer's complaint about wait time, wrong food, and cold temperature. Acknowledge, apologise, and offer a solution.
content.example_response: Good evening â€” I'm the manager. I understand you've had some issues this evening and I want to sincerely apologise for that. A 45-minute wait is absolutely not what we aim for and I can see that we fell short of our standards tonight. I also apologise for the mix-up with your order â€” receiving the wrong dish is completely unacceptable and I take full responsibility for that. And the food being cold on top of that â€” I'm truly sorry. Here's what I'd like to do. First, I'm having the correct dish â€” the paneer tikka masala â€” prepared fresh for you right now. It'll be ready in about 10 minutes. Second, I'd like to offer you and your companion complimentary desserts as an apology for the inconvenience. And finally, I'm removing the main course from your bill tonight. You've been a loyal customer and I want to make sure you leave tonight feeling that we've done right by you. I'll personally check on your table once the food arrives. Once again, I'm genuinely sorry for your experience this evening.
key_phrases: here's what I'd like to do
I want to make sure you leave satisfied

content.text:
Subject: Complaint Regarding Stay â€” Room 412 â€” 10-12 October

Dear Manager,

I am writing ___ (1) a complaint regarding my recent stay at your hotel on the above dates.

___ (2), I would like to highlight that my room was not ___ (3) â€” I specifically requested a non-smoking room during booking, yet the room had a strong smell of smoke. I was eventually ___ (4) to Room 608, which I appreciate.

Secondly, I ___ (5) to have been incorrectly charged for an airport pickup service that I did not use. ___ (6), this was resolved at reception, but I would ask that you review your booking system to prevent similar errors.

I was generally satisfied with the ___ (7) of your staff in resolving these issues. However, given the ___ (8) I experienced, I ___ (9) appreciate a partial refund or a complimentary upgrade on my next visit.

___ (10) forward to your response.

Yours sincerely,
Kavya Iyer

content.title: Lesson 11 complete â€” Complaints and requests in person
key_points: Structure: explain problem + why it matters + request a solution
'I'm afraid there's a problem with...' softens the opening
'I think there may have been a mistake with...' = polite framing
Request: 'Could you possibly...?' / 'I'd appreciate it if...'
Staff response: acknowledge â†’ apologise â†’ offer solution immediately
Never blame the customer or say 'That's not my problem'
Stay calm and specific â€” vague or emotional complaints are harder to resolve
